headline: Elon Musk summoned by French prosecutors after tense 15-month probe into X
subhead: Paris prosecutors have been investigating a wide range of alleged abuses by X and its owner since January 2025. The billionaire has been summoned for a voluntary interview on Monday.

French housing crisis is straining relations between landlords and tenants
subhead: Tensions have risen at every stage of the landlord-tenant relationship amid a nationwide housing shortage.

headline: The undeniable international success of Languedoc wines
subhead: Appellations from the world's largest wine-growing region sell 40% of their production abroad. Thanks to strong ambassadors, wines from the area are now found in nearly half the countries on the planet.

headline: 'Come Closer' by Tomora: When the Chemical Brothers' electronic fever meets Aurora's grace
subhead: British musician Tom Rowlands, one half of the Chemical Brothers, and Norwegian singer Aurora blend their musical worlds on their stunning first joint album.

headline: How video games are shaping modern warfare
subhead: Drone piloting with console controllers, first-person shooter games used to recruit soldiers: For the past 60 years, the video game industry and military leadership have been locked in a relationship of mutual dependence.

Ireland, the weak link in Europe's defense
subhead: Irish neutrality, long seen as a guarantee of independence from its former British colonizer, has been eroding under European and Atlantic pressures. After decades of naval weakness, Dublin has committed to strengthening its navy.

headline: The turbulent life of Pavel Talankin, the Oscar-winning director in the Russian government's crosshairs
subhead: The global release of 'Mr. Nobody Against Putin,' which exposes Russian war propaganda to primary school students in the Urals, brought the film's director, Pavel Talankin, to public attention. But the Kremlin labeled him a 'foreign agent,' increasing the threats against the filmmaker, now living in exile in Prague.

headline: Can we detox our bodies from pollutants like 'forever chemicals'?
subhead: Scientists are testing methods to remove certain pollutants from the human body. These methods can be both invasive and costly, and do not prevent renewed exposure to the most widespread environmental toxins.

headline: Vincent Bolloré's troubling grip on French publishing
'The Rassemblement National's social image is largely an illusion'
'By firing a publisher, Bolloré united writers, who are usually unpredictable and solitary'
